2025-2026 Academic Year Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility Call for Applications is Now Open!

2025-2026 Academic Year Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility Call for Applications

Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility – Application Timeline

Process

Start Date

End Date

Erasmus+ Traineeship Applications

February 6, 2026 (09:00)

February 23, 2026 (17:30)

Submission of Acceptance Letter for +10 Bonus Points (

)

February 6, 2026 (09:00)

February 23, 2026 (17:30)

Final Deadline for Submitting Acceptance Letter (

no bonus points

)

–

Optional Foreign Language Exam (organized by the School of Foreign Languages)

February 25, 2026

–

Announcement of Results

–

Dear Students,

Erasmus

Student Traineeship Mobility

refers to a mobility activity in which a student enrolled at a higher education institution undertakes a traineeship abroad in a company, research institute, laboratory, or any relevant organization or institution, in a field related to their academic studies.

Within the scope of Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility, applications for the 2025-2026 Academic Year will be received between

February 6, 2026 (09:00)

and

February 23, 2026 (17:30)

through two online application systems:

MyKHAS and E-Government (E-Devlet)

.

Please read the application guidelines carefully before submitting your application.

Applications must be completed through

two online systems

:

e-Devlet (E-Government)

and

MyKHAS

.

Applications submitted only via MyKHAS will not be considered valid.

You must first complete your application through

e-Devlet

, and then finalize your application via

MyKHAS

.

Students who do not complete the e-Devlet application will be considered

ineligible

.

The minimum mobility duration for Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility is

2 months.

Mobilities shorter than this period will not be considered valid.

Application Steps

Applications must be completed through both online systems: MyKHAS and E-Devlet (Turna Portal).

Applications submitted through only one system will be considered invalid. The deadline for completing the application and uploading documents is

February 23, 2026 at 17:30

. Applications with missing documents or incomplete submissions will be considered

invalid

.

Application links:

MyKHAS:

https://my.khas.edu.tr/erasmus/erasmus_staj_programi

https://turnaportal.ua.gov.tr/

Application Documents:

1. Application Form (to be filled through both systems –

E-devlet

&

Mykhas

)

2. 1 original Transcript from Student Affairs (English or Turkish)

3. Acceptance Letter (

If you have it

)

Students who upload their acceptance letter through the application system during the application period (February 6 – February 23, 2026) will receive

+10 bonus points

.

After the application system closes (after February 23, 2026), acceptance letters may still be submitted via email until March 6, 2026; however,

no bonus points will be awarded

for documents submitted after the application deadline.

Announcement of Application Results

The application results will be officially announced following the evaluation process.

Applicants will be ranked based on their Erasmus score, regardless of their department. Grant allocation will be determined according to the total grant budget allocated to our University.

Results will be published through the Turna Portal application system, and applicants will also be informed via e-mail.

Deadline for Submission of Acceptance Letter

📅

📧

Email:

[email protected]

Students who are eligible based on the initial evaluation but have not yet submitted an acceptance letter must send their official acceptance document by the deadline above.

Students who fail to submit an acceptance letter by the final deadline will be considered as having incomplete applications, and their placement rights will be cancelled.

The acceptance letter must be issued on the host institution’s official letterhead and include a signature and stamp.

Application Criteria:

Minimum 1-year Grade Point Average (

Preparatory class students and first-year undergraduate students are not eligible to apply.

)

Grade Point Average:

Graduate (Master’s) & PhD students: Minimum 2.50

·      English Proficiency Criteria: They must meet one of the minimum English proficiency criteria listed below and the grades of the following English exams must be entered into the system at the Student Affairs’s Office before the application and they must be submitted during the application:

·

·

·      English Language Proficiency Score is created by taking the average of the last 2 courses from EL courses.

·

KHAS 103-105: CB and above

·      Students who cannot provide the required proficiency in the above courses can also submit the following valid exam results they have taken in the last 5 years:

·

TOEFL IBT: 78

PTE Academic: 60

English exam conducted by our university

Erasmus Score:

It is calculated by taking the arithmetic average of GPA and English Language Proficiency Score.

However, the following aspects are also taken into account in the evaluation of applications:

Criteria

Weighted Score

Academic achievement level

50% (out of total 100 points)

Language proficiency level

50% (out of total 100 points)

Children of martyrs and veterans

+15 points*

Students with disabilities (with documented proof)

+10 points**

Students under protection, care, or accommodation decisions within the scope of Social Services Law No. 2828 and Child Protection Law No. 5395

+10 points***

Submission of internship acceptance letter during application

+10 points

Students (or first-degree relatives) receiving disaster victim aid from AFAD

+10 points****

Internships focused on developing digital skills (DOTs)

+5 points*****

Applying within the scope of compulsory internship

+5 points

Previous participation in the same type of mobility (with or without grant)

-10 points

Participating in mobility in the country of citizenship

-10 points

Being selected for mobility but failing to submit a waiver (cancellation) notice within the specified time

-10 points

Applying to two mobility types simultaneously (reduction applied to preferred mobility type)

-10 points

Not attending meetings/trainings organized by the higher education institution without valid excuse (applied to next application)

-10 points

Declaring participation in language exam but failing to attend without valid excuse (applied to next application)

-5 points

*

Combat veterans and their spouses and children; the spouses and children of war martyrs; as well as, in accordance with Article 21 of the Anti-Terror Law No. 3713 dated April 12, 1991, the spouses and children of public officials who have been injured, disabled, killed, or murdered as a result of terrorist acts while performing their duties domestically or abroad (or due to their former duties); and pursuant to Article 7 of Decree Law No. 667 dated July 23, 2016, the spouses and children of those who lost their lives or were disabled as a result of the coup attempt and terrorist acts on July 15, 2016, and related subsequent acts, shall be given priority when applying for Erasmus+ student mobility.

**

For prioritization, the student must submit a Disability Health Board Report issued in accordance with the “Regulation on Disability Assessment for Adults” published in the Official Gazette dated February 20, 2019, and numbered 30692.

***

For prioritization, the student must submit an official document issued by the Ministry of Family and Social Policies confirming that a protection, care, or accommodation decision has been granted under Law No. 2828.

****

This additional point is applied only once for each study cycle (Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD) and for each project type (KA131–KA171). If the student participates in the mobility, the additional point will not be applied in subsequent applications. If the student does not participate in the mobility (provided that the withdrawal is made within the waiver period or due to force majeure), the additional point will continue to be applied in future applications.

*****

Internships that involve one or more of the following activities are considered within this scope: digital marketing (e.g., social media management, web analytics), digital graphics, mechanical and architectural design; application, software, coding or website development; installation, maintenance, and management of information systems and networks, cybersecurity, data analytics, data mining and visualization; programming, robotics, and artificial intelligence training. General customer service, request creation, data entry, or routine office tasks are not considered within this scope.

*** Please note that our

quota is limited to 10 students who will receive a grant

.

Students not awarded a grant can still go to their mobilities without funding if they are eligible.

Grant Amounts:

Programme Countries

Hosting Countries for Mobility

Monthly Grant for Traineeships (Euro)

1st and 2nd Group Countries

Germany, Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Greek Cypriot Administration of Southern Cyprus, Netherlands, Ireland, Spain, Sweden, Italy, Iceland, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Greece.

750

3rd Group Countries

Bulgaria, Croatia, North Macedonia, Lithuania, Hungary, Poland, Romania, Serbia, Türkiye.

600

Travel Support Calculations

In order to contribute to students’ travel expenses, the travel grant must be calculated using the distance calculator provided by the European Commission. The kilometer distance between the starting point of the mobility and the location where the activity takes place must be determined, and the travel grant should be calculated according to the table below.

The distance calculated using the distance band calculator corresponds to the

round-trip distance

, and the amount specified in the table should

not

be multiplied by two.

Green travel:

This refers to travel carried out using low-carbon emission public transport. Bus, train, and carsharing are considered part of green travel.

Additional Grant Support in Student / Recent Graduate Mobility

Additional grant support will be provided to participants with limited opportunities in addition to the grant they are entitled to receive. In order for this grant to be awarded, the participant with limited opportunities must be defined as an individual with economic and social disadvantages and fall within the sub-categories listed below.

Eligible categories:

Individuals subject to Law No. 2828 (those for whom a protection, care, or accommodation decision has been taken by the Ministry of Family and Social Services under Law No. 2828)

Students for whom a protection, care, or accommodation decision has been taken within the scope of Child Protection Law No. 5395

Individuals receiving orphan/survivor pensions

Spouses and children of martyrs/veterans, as well as veterans themselves

*

Students who submit documentation proving that they or their family or guardian receive support from a public institution (such as municipalities, ministries, Social Assistance and Solidarity Foundations, Directorate General of Foundations, Turkish Red Crescent, AFAD, etc.) at the time of application

Students with disabilities

**

Individuals who themselves, their parents, or guardians receive disability or social assistance allowance under Law No. 2022 dated 01.07.1976 concerning the provision of monthly allowances to needy, weak, and destitute Turkish citizens aged 65 or above and to disabled and needy Turkish citizens

***

Individuals who themselves or their first-degree relatives receive disaster assistance from AFAD

****

Scholarships from the Credit and Dormitories Institution (KYK) and similar scholarships, merit-based scholarships, and one-time financial aids are

not

considered within the scope of financial assistance under this category.

Students who meet the above criteria may receive Additional Grant Support, provided that they request it and submit the necessary documentation, in the amounts specified below depending on the type of mobility:

*

Combat veterans and their spouses and children; the spouses and children of war martyrs; as well as, in accordance with Article 21 of the Anti-Terror Law No. 3713 dated April 12, 1991, the spouses and children of public officials who were injured, disabled, killed, or murdered as a result of terrorist acts while performing their duties domestically or abroad (or due to their former duties); and pursuant to Article 7 of Decree Law No. 667 dated July 23, 2016, the spouses and children of individuals who lost their lives or were disabled as a result of the coup attempt and terrorist acts carried out on July 15, 2016, and related subsequent acts, shall be given priority in Erasmus+ student mobility applications.

**

Individuals with disabilities who have a disability rate of at least 50%, documented by a Disability Health Board Report issued in accordance with the “Regulation on Disability Assessment for Adults” published in the Official Gazette dated February 20, 2019 (No. 30692).

***

https://www.mevzuat.gov.tr/MevzuatMetin/1.5.2022.pdf

****

IMPORTANT NOTE:

The following organizations are not eligible to host Erasmus+ traineeship activities:

European Union institutions and EU agencies (see:

https://europa.eu/european-union/about-eu/institutions-bodies_en

)

Organizations responsible for the management of EU programmes, including National Agencies.

·      Our graduates can also benefit from mobility when they apply before they graduate.

However, the internship right must be completed within 12 months following graduation.

·      Within the scope of Erasmus internship program,

students are granted a maximum of 2 months.

·      Payments are made in two installments, 70% of the total grant before going and 30% when returning. A maximum of 2 months grant is given for Internship Mobility.

We wish you luck.

Erasmus and Exchange Office
